title: Onuris hatcheriana
taxonomy: Onuris hatcheriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Capparaceae. It belongs to the genus Onuris. The plant is native to North America. Onuris hatcheriana is closely related to other plants in the Capparaceae family, which are also known as the caper family. It is a unique species within this family, and its exact classification is still being researched and refined.

intro: Onuris hatcheriana, also known as Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Onuris hatcheriana include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues.
